### Post by gaegi on 2008-09-03
installed nvidia drivers ,system requires restart for enabling.when restarted it tells to configure graphics card manually ,on setting desired resolution & checking it turns screen into  grey & then resets itself to 800*600.



MY xorg.conf is

# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by failsafeDexconf, using
# values from the debconf database and some overrides to use vesa mode.
#
# You should use dexconf or another such tool for creating a "real" xorg.conf
# For example:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Boardname	"NVIDIA GeForce 6 Series"
	Busid		"PCI:0:13:0"
	Driver		"nv"
	Screen	0
	Vendorname	"NVIDIA"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
	Vendorname	"Generic LCD Display"
	Modelname	"LCD Panel 1440x900"
	Horizsync	31.5-56.0
	Vertrefresh	56.0 - 65.0
  modeline  "800x600@56" 36.0 800 824 896 1024 600 601 603 625 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "800x600@60" 40.0 800 840 968 1056 600 601 605 628 +hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x768@60" 80.14 1280 1344 1480 1680 768 769 772 795 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x720@60" 74.48 1280 1336 1472 1664 720 721 724 746 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1280x800@60" 83.46 1280 1344 1480 1680 800 801 804 828 -hsync +vsync
  modeline  "1440x900@60" 106.47 1440 1520 1672 1904 900 901 904 932 -hsync +vsync
	Gamma	1.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Defaultdepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Depth	24
		Modes		"1440x900@60"	"1280x800@60"	"1280x720@60"	"1280x768@60"	"800x600@60"	"800x600@56"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen 0 "Default Screen" 0 0
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
	Load		"GLcore"
	Load		"v4l"
EndSection
Section "ServerFlags"
EndSection

### Post by xx77aBs on 2008-09-03
I'm not sure, but I think that drivers doesn't work with your graphic card. You should try to configure them or maybe search for other drivers (if there are any) ...

---

### Post by tuxxy on 2008-09-03
What video card are you using, you could download envyng from the repos and try and install using this application.

```
sudo apt-get install envy-gtk
```

---

### Post by Zimmer on 2008-09-03
Check the xorg log to see if the monitor is being detected  or being detected badly...
And, BTW, if you installed "NVIDIA" proprietary drivers should your driver statement contain "nvidia" and NOT "nv"  ???

You could also try  in the Screen section of xorg.conf
Option         "UseEdid" "False"
if the monitor resolutions still give you problems
and if you have the correct modeline settings in the config.....
It will not necessarily work, I am still trying to fathom out how the xorg config and the latest X interact with my own monitor issues.
This may be of interest, too
[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/xorg-server/+bug/194760](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/xorg-server/+bug/194760)

---
